grounding_source_rows: human_disagreements rows 66, 125, 126, 33; taxonomy Business & Corporate descriptions
grounding_note: Business & Corporate routing turns on entity/industry facts: nonprofit status (501c3), liquor licensing (Restaurant/OLCC), franchise agreements, securities offerings, marijuana licensing, or a sale/dissolution transaction. Rows 66/125/126 all split because the description omitted the entity's nature. 'I need a business lawyer' is the generic opening; the entity fact routes it.

scenarios.opening_query: I'm starting an organization with two friends and we need help setting it up properly.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Business and Corporate > General (contracts, entities) || Business and Corporate > Non-Profit
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's a charity for veterans' housing — we need 501(c)(3) status so donations are deductible.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's a veterans' housing charity — we need 501(c)(3) status for deductible donations.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the entity is charitable/nonprofit or for-profit
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a for-profit landscaping company — we need an LLC and an operating agreement between the three of us.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's a for-profit landscaping company — LLC plus operating agreement for the three of us.
scenarios.vetting_note: Nonprofit formation has distinct regulation and its own label; 'organization' conceals which.

scenarios.opening_query: My restaurant is facing a regulatory problem that could shut us down within the month.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Business and Corporate > Restaurants/OLCC || Administrative Law > General (State)
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's not the liquor license — the state agriculture department is threatening our license over a food-safety citation we believe was erroneous.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Not the liquor license — it's the state ag department threatening us over a food-safety citation we think is wrong.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: which regulator and license is threatened
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's the OLCC — they moved to suspend our liquor license over two alleged underage sales.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's the OLCC — moving to suspend our liquor license over two alleged underage sales.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 66's restaurant thread: OLCC matters have a dedicated label; other agencies route to admin law.

scenarios.opening_query: I'm buying into an existing business and the paperwork they sent is way over my head.
scenarios.initial: Business and Corporate > Sale Of Business
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Business and Corporate > Sale Of Business || Business and Corporate > Franchise
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's a national sandwich franchise — the documents are a franchise disclosure document and franchise agreement, not a normal purchase.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's a national sandwich franchise — the paperwork is an FDD and franchise agreement, not a regular purchase.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the transaction is a franchise rather than an independent business sale
scenarios.counterfactual_final: Business and Corporate > Sale Of Business
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's an independent local shop — a straight asset purchase from the retiring owner.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's an independent shop — straight asset purchase from a retiring owner.
scenarios.vetting_note: FDD documents invoke franchise-specific law; 'buying into a business' hides the distinction.

scenarios.opening_query: We want to raise money from people we know to expand the company and need to do it right.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Business and Corporate > General (contracts, entities) || Business and Corporate > Securities
scenarios.hidden_fact: We're planning to sell actual equity shares to about 20 friends-and-family investors — I've been warned that's a securities offering with real rules.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We'd sell actual equity to about 20 friends-and-family investors — I'm told that's a regulated securities offering.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the fundraising involves selling securities
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a simple loan from my father-in-law — we just need a proper promissory note drafted.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's one loan from my father-in-law — we just need a proper promissory note.
scenarios.vetting_note: Equity-vs-loan is the securities trigger; the taxonomy's Securities label is issuer-side, matching this.

scenarios.opening_query: My business partner and I are splitting up and it's getting complicated fast.
scenarios.initial: Business and Corporate > Sale Of Business
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Business and Corporate > Sale Of Business || Bankruptcy > Business || Business and Corporate > General (contracts, entities)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The company can't pay its debts — the split only works if the LLC itself goes through bankruptcy while we separate.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The company's insolvent — the split really means putting the LLC through bankruptcy while we separate.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the entity is insolvent and needs bankruptcy alongside the buyout
scenarios.counterfactual_final: Business and Corporate > Sale Of Business
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The company's healthy — she's buying me out over three years and we need the buyout papered.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The company's healthy — she's buying me out over three years and it needs papering.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 33 verbatim (dissolving LLC + bankruptcy question); solvency is the routing fact.

scenarios.opening_query: Our board got a letter from a lawyer about the site next to our property and we don't know his angle.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Business and Corporate > General (contracts, entities) || Business and Corporate > Environmental || Business and Corporate > Non-Profit
scenarios.hidden_fact: The site is a Superfund cleanup area and the letter concerns contamination migration — this is environmental liability territory.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The site is a Superfund area and the letter is about contamination migration — environmental liability territory.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether environmental contamination liability is the subject
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The letter turned out to be a routine easement-purchase offer for utility access across our lot.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It turned out to be a routine easement purchase offer for utility access.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 125 verbatim (nonprofit board, Superfund site, unknown intentions); contamination fact routes it.

scenarios.opening_query: I'm opening a dispensary and my lease and licensing questions are stacking up.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Business and Corporate > General (contracts, entities) || Business and Corporate > Marijuana Business
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's a licensed cannabis retail operation — OLCC cannabis rules, banking restrictions, and the landlord's federal-illegality clause all collide.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's cannabis retail — OLCC cannabis licensing, banking limits, and a federal-illegality lease clause all collide.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the business is a regulated marijuana operation
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: 'Dispensary' is our optical shop's name — it's ordinary retail: lease review and an LLC.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's actually our optical shop's branding — ordinary retail lease and LLC work.
scenarios.vetting_note: Cannabis-specific regulatory stack has a dedicated label; playful counterfactual keeps the pair honest.

scenarios.opening_query: Another company is refusing to pay our invoices and the amount is now six figures.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Business and Corporate > Litigation || International Law > Business And Corporate
scenarios.hidden_fact: The customer is in South Korea and our contract says disputes go to arbitration there — cross-border enforcement is the whole problem.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They're in South Korea and the contract sends disputes to arbitration there — cross-border enforcement is the whole problem.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the counterparty and forum are foreign
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: They're two towns over — a straightforward domestic collection suit on the contract.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They're two towns over — straightforward domestic contract suit.
scenarios.vetting_note: Foreign-forum fact reroutes commercial collection into international practice.

scenarios.opening_query: I run a small farm operation and I need business advice about a deal I've been offered.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Business and Corporate > General (contracts, entities) || Business and Corporate > Agriculture/Farm
scenarios.hidden_fact: The deal is a multi-year crop contract with a processor, tangled with water allotments and a farm-lease assignment — it's agricultural law through and through.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's a multi-year crop contract with a processor, plus water allotments and a farm-lease assignment — pure agricultural law.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the transaction involves agriculture-specific arrangements
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The deal is just selling our delivery van fleet to a neighbor's company — ordinary contract work.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's just selling our van fleet to a neighbor's company — ordinary contract stuff.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 140's agriculture routing; crop/water facts pull toward the specialized label.

scenarios.opening_query: A guy is making money off videos of my performances without any agreement with me.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Intellectual Property > Entertainment || Business and Corporate > Entertainment || Intellectual Property > Trademark/Copyright
scenarios.hidden_fact: He's my former manager — there WAS a contract, and the fight is whether his expired management deal still gives him video rights. It's a contract fight, not raw infringement.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He's my ex-manager — there was a contract, and the fight is whether his expired deal still covers video rights. It's contractual.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an underlying entertainment contract governs the use
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He's a stranger who records shows and sells clips — no agreement has ever existed between us.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He's a stranger selling clips of my shows — no agreement has ever existed.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 126's IP-vs-business entertainment split: contract-existence is the boundary fact.
